solution:
def findNthDigit(n: int) -> int:
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    elif n < 0:
        return -1
    else:
        return str(n)[-1]

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution uses a simple if-elif-else statement to handle the cases where n is 0 or negative. For positive n, it returns the last digit of the string representation of n using indexing [-1]. The time complexity is O(1) as the operation is constant time, and the space complexity is also O(1) as the function only uses a constant amount of space.
